summary: ByteDance Seed surfaced the clearest product artifact of the day with Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena and a new Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM aimed at real-time conversational interaction. On the research side, LMGenDrive proposed an end-to-end driving stack that combines multimodal understanding with generative world modeling for long-tail autonomy, while the paper "Large Language Models Generate Harmful Content Using a Distinct, Unified Mechanism" argues harmful generation may arise from a single internal mechanism with implications for alignment and jailbreak defense.

The company also introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, positioned around attentive listening, interference suppression, and more natural real-time interaction.
ByteDance paired the launches with a 2027 foundation-model campus recruiting push, a signal that it is scaling talent investment alongside model releases.

top_topics.title: Large Language Models Generate Harmful Content Using a Distinct, Unified Mechanism
top_topics.summary: Large language models (LLMs) undergo alignment training to avoid harmful behaviors, yet the resulting safeguards remain brittle: jailbreaks routinely bypass them, and fine-tuning on narrow domains can induce ``emergent misalignment'' that generalizes broadly. Whether this brittleness reflects a fundamental lack of coherent internal organization for harmfulness remains unclear. Here we use targeted weight pruning as a causal intervention to probe the internal organization of harmfulness in LLMs. We find that harmful content generation depends on a compact set of weights that are general acro...

items.title: Large Language Models Generate Harmful Content Using a Distinct, Unified Mechanism
items.summary: Large language models (LLMs) undergo alignment training to avoid harmful behaviors, yet the resulting safeguards remain brittle: jailbreaks routinely bypass them, and fine-tuning on narrow domains can induce ``emergent misalignment'' that generalizes broadly. Whether this brittleness reflects a fundamental lack of coherent internal organization for harmfulness remains unclear. Here we use targeted weight pruning as a causal intervention to probe the internal organization of harmfulness in LLMs. We find that harmful content generation depends on a compact set of weights that are general acro...

metadata.comment: Shows via causal weight pruning that harmful generation relies on a compact, shared, and compressible internal mechanism distinct from benign capabilities.

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: Large language models (LLMs) undergo alignment training to avoid harmful behaviors, yet the resulting safeguards remain brittle: jailbreaks routinely bypass them, and fine-tuning on narrow domains can induce ``emergent misalignment'' that generalizes broadly. Whether this brittleness reflects a fundamental lack of coherent internal organization for harmfulness remains unclear. Here we use targeted weight pruning as a causal intervention to probe the internal organization of harmfulness in LLMs. We find that harmful content generation depends on a compact set of weights that are general acro...
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: The work could influence safety roadmaps by suggesting jailbreak resistance and harmful-output mitigation may require targeting a shared internal mechanism rather than patching individual behaviors.
top_topics.HEADLINE: "Large Language Models Generate Harmful Content Using a Distinct, Unified Mechanism" Probes Alignment Failure Modes
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: The paper argues harmful content generation in aligned LLMs may stem from a distinct, unified mechanism rather than many unrelated failure modes.
Its framing speaks directly to persistent industry problems including jailbreaks and emergent misalignment after narrow fine-tuning.
For model developers, the implication is strategic: mechanistic interpretability and representation-level interventions may matter more than adding more surface-level refusal training.

top_topics.title: LMGenDrive: Bridging Multimodal Understanding and Generative World Modeling for End-to-End Driving
top_topics.summary: Recent years have seen remarkable progress in autonomous driving, yet generalization to long-tail and open-world scenarios remains a major bottleneck for large-scale deployment. To address this challenge, some works use LLMs and VLMs for vision-language understanding and reasoning, enabling vehicles to interpret rare and safety-critical situations when generating actions. Others study generative world models to capture the spatio-temporal evolution of driving scenes, allowing agents to imagine possible futures before acting. Inspired by human intelligence, which unifies understanding and im...

top_topics.SHORT_COMMENT: Recent years have seen remarkable progress in autonomous driving, yet generalization to long-tail and open-world scenarios remains a major bottleneck for large-scale deployment. To address this challenge, some works use LLMs and VLMs for vision-language understanding and reasoning, enabling vehicles to interpret rare and safety-critical situations when generating actions. Others study generative world models to capture the spatio-temporal evolution of driving scenes, allowing agents to imagine possible futures before acting. Inspired by human intelligence, which unifies understanding and im...
top_topics.WHY_IT_MATTERS: Autonomy investors and automotive R&D leaders should watch whether LLM- and VLM-based world models can improve long-tail driving generalization beyond conventional end-to-end stacks.
top_topics.HEADLINE: LMGenDrive Paper Targets End-to-End Driving with Multimodal Understanding and World Modeling
KEY_TAKEAWAYS: LMGenDrive is framed around bridging multimodal understanding with generative world modeling, aiming to handle open-world and long-tail driving scenarios more robustly.
The paper explicitly targets a core deployment bottleneck in autonomous driving: poor generalization outside the training distribution.
If validated, the approach would strengthen the case for integrating language-and-vision reasoning modules directly into production driving systems rather than treating them as offline copilots.

topics.title: Nexus: Same Pretraining Loss, Better Downstream Generalization via Common Minima
topics.summary: Pretraining is the cornerstone of Large Language Models (LLMs), dominating the vast majority of computational budget and data to serve as the primary engine for their capabilities. During pretraining, LLMs acquire foundational knowledge from an unprecedentedly massive and diverse data sources, encompassing a vast array of domains such as general language, mathematics, code, and complex reasoning. In this work, we investigate an interesting geometric question regarding the converged state of pretraining: Does the model converge to a common minimizer across all data sources (e.g., \cref{fig:c...

topics.title: Physics-guided surrogate learning enables zero-shot control of turbulent wings
topics.summary: Turbulent boundary layers over aerodynamic surfaces are a major source of aircraft drag, yet their control remains challenging due to multiscale dynamics and spatial variability, particularly under adverse pressure gradients. Reinforcement learning has outperformed state-of-the-art strategies in canonical flows, but its application to realistic geometries is limited by computational cost and transferability. Here we show that these limitations can be overcome by exploiting local structures of wall-bounded turbulence. Policies are trained in turbulent channel flows matched to wing boundary-l...

topics.title: RAMP: Hybrid DRL for Online Learning of Numeric Action Models
topics.summary: Automated planning algorithms require an action model specifying the preconditions and effects of each action, but obtaining such a model is often hard. Learning action models from observations is feasible, but existing algorithms for numeric domains are offline, requiring expert traces as input. We propose the Reinforcement learning, Action Model learning, and Planning (RAMP) strategy for learning numeric planning action models online via interactions with the environment. RAMP simultaneously trains a Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) policy, learns a numeric action model from past interac...

topics.title: Re-Mask and Redirect: Exploiting Denoising Irreversibility in Diffusion Language Models
topics.summary: Diffusion-based language models (dLLMs) generate text by iteratively denoising masked token sequences. We show that their safety alignment rests on a single fragile assumption: that the denoising schedule is monotonic and committed tokens are never re-evaluated. Safety-aligned dLLMs commit refusal tokens within the first 8-16 of 64 denoising steps, and the schedule treats these commitments as permanent. A trivial two-step intervention - re-masking these tokens and injecting a 12-token affirmative prefix - achieves 76.1% ASR on HarmBench (n=159, Lg=128) against LLaDA-8B-Instruct and 81.8% AS...

items.title: Re-Mask and Redirect: Exploiting Denoising Irreversibility in Diffusion Language Models
items.summary: Diffusion-based language models (dLLMs) generate text by iteratively denoising masked token sequences. metadata.comment: Exposes a structural vulnerability in diffusion language models by showing safety depends on irreversible token commitments in the denoising schedule.

topics.title: SafeAdapt: Provably Safe Policy Updates in Deep Reinforcement Learning
topics.summary: Safety guarantees are a prerequisite to the deployment of reinforcement learning (RL) agents in safety-critical tasks. Often, deployment environments exhibit non-stationary dynamics or are subject to changing performance goals, requiring updates to the learned policy. This leads to a fundamental challenge: how to update an RL policy while preserving its safety properties on previously encountered tasks? The majority of current approaches either do not provide formal guarantees or verify policy safety only a posteriori. We propose a novel a priori approach to safe policy updates in continual...

items.title: SafeAdapt: Provably Safe Policy Updates in Deep Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: Safety guarantees are a prerequisite to the deployment of reinforcement learning (RL) agents in safety-critical tasks. Often, deployment environments exhibit non-stationary dynamics or are subject to changing performance goals, requiring updates to the learned policy. This leads to a fundamental challenge: how to update an RL policy while preserving its safety properties on previously encountered tasks? The majority of current approaches either do not provide formal guarantees or verify policy safety only a posteriori. We propose a novel a priori approach to safe policy updates in continual...
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Provides a provably safe policy-update method for continual RL by projecting arbitrary updates onto a certified Rashomon set that preserves prior safety constraints.
metadata.primary_topic_label: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

topics.title: TensorHub: Scalable and Elastic Weight Transfer for LLM RL Training
topics.summary: Modern LLM reinforcement learning (RL) workloads require a highly efficient weight transfer system to scale training across heterogeneous computational resources. However, existing weight transfer approaches either fail to provide flexibility for dynamically scaling clusters or incur fundamental data movement overhead, resulting in poor performance. We introduce Reference-Oriented Storage (ROS), a new storage abstraction for RL weight transfer that exploits the highly replicated model weights in place. ROS presents the illusion that certain versions of the model weights are stored and can b...

topics.title: Truncated Rectified Flow Policy for Reinforcement Learning with One-Step Sampling
topics.summary: Maximum entropy reinforcement learning (MaxEnt RL) has become a standard framework for sequential decision making, yet its standard Gaussian policy parameterization is inherently unimodal, limiting its ability to model complex multimodal action distributions. This limitation has motivated increasing interest in generative policies based on diffusion and flow matching as more expressive alternatives. However, incorporating such policies into MaxEnt RL is challenging for two main reasons: the likelihood and entropy of continuous-time generative policies are generally intractable, and multi-st...

items.summary: Maximum entropy reinforcement learning (MaxEnt RL) has become a standard framework for sequential decision making, yet its standard Gaussian policy parameterization is inherently unimodal, limiting its ability to model complex multimodal action distributions. However, incorporating such policies into MaxEnt RL is challenging for two main reasons: the likelihood and entropy of continuous-time generative policies are generally intractable, and multi-st...
tags: World Models, Exploration, and Open-Ended Reinforcement Learning

metadata.comment: Makes flow-based MaxEnt RL practical via a truncated rectified-flow policy that supports tractable entropy optimization and one-step multimodal action sampling.

topics.title: Dynamic sparsity in tree-structured feed-forward layers at scale
topics.summary: A paper titled 'Dynamic sparsity in tree-structured feed-forward layers at scale' is trending. It examines scalable tree-structured feed-forward layers for hard conditional computation without a separate router.

items.title: Dynamic sparsity in tree-structured feed-forward layers at scale
items.summary: Broadly important today: Shows scalable tree-structured feed-forward layers can realize hard conditional computation without a separate router, and analyzes emergent auto-pruning dynamics.

items.title: Spectral-Transport Stability and Benign Overfitting in Interpolating Learning
items.summary: It proposes a fairly original unified framework linking stability, spectral geometry, noise alignment, and benign overfitting.

items.title: Loom: A Scalable Analytical Neural Computer Architecture
items.summary: It proposes an unusual analytically constructed looped-transformer computer architecture that feels like a new paradigm direction.
items.spotlight_comment: It proposes an unusual analytically constructed looped-transformer computer architecture that feels like a new paradigm direction.

items.title: StructRL: Recovering Dynamic Programming Structure from Learning Dynamics in Distributional Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: It proposes an unusual perspective that recovers dynamic-programming structure from RL learning traces, which could seed a new line of foundational RL methods.

items.title: Toward Hardware-Agnostic Quadrupedal World Models via Morphology Conditioning
items.summary: Broadly important today: Builds a morphology-conditioned world model that explicitly factors robot embodiment into dynamics for zero-shot transfer across quadruped hardware.

items.title: Sharp description of local minima in the loss landscape of high-dimensional two-layer ReLU neural networks
items.summary: Broadly important today: Gives a sharp low-dimensional characterization of local minima in two-layer ReLU networks and links them to attractive fixed points of SGD dynamics.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Gives a sharp low-dimensional characterization of local minima in two-layer ReLU networks and links them to attractive fixed points of SGD dynamics.

items.title: OASIS: Online Activation Subspace Learning for Memory-Efficient Training
items.summary: Broadly important today: Learns an online low-dimensional activation subspace to cut activation, gradient, and optimizer-state memory during training.
items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Learns an online low-dimensional activation subspace to cut activation, gradient, and optimizer-state memory during training.
